justification: All core pass criteria met. PM/Legal contradiction is explicitly identified and formally escalated (escalation #1) with three resolution options and a recommendation. Wrong-address exception from Finance captured as BR-6 with testable assertion. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing tension is partially surfaced via escalation #2 (overlapping refund triggers) and both BR-7 (3 business days) and BR-8 (automatic refund) are extracted, though the specific timing conflict is not called out as sharply as ideal. Clock-start ambiguity is noted as an assumption ('from order placement, not from pickup') and listed in low_confidence_areas, but not formally escalated as a stakeholder disagreement — this is the weakest area. 9 business rules extracted with reasonable invariant classifications. 2 formal escalations with options and recommendations. Confidence at 40 is appropriately low given the contradictions.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is thoroughly escalated with three concrete resolution options and a pragmatic recommendation (Option C hybrid approach)
9 well-structured business rules extracted, each with invariant justification and testable assertions — exceeds the 5-rule minimum significantly
Wrong-address refund exception from Finance correctly captured as BR-6 with fraud/abuse prevention framing
Confidence at 40 with clear reasoning about why the spec cannot be finalized — correctly identifies the contradiction as blocking
Excellent edge cases including double-refund triggers, boundary conditions (exactly 10km), driver rating drop mid-delivery, and wrong-address dispute resolution
Ubiquitous language includes 'Goodwill Refund' term, correctly adopting Legal's framing rather than PM's guarantee language
Clock-start ambiguity (order placement vs pickup) is assumed rather than escalated — the PM/Ops disagreement on when the 2-hour window begins deserved its own formal escalation

Auto-refund (CS requirement) vs 3-business-day processing (Finance SLA) tension is not explicitly surfaced as a conflict — both rules are captured but the contradiction between 'automatic' and '3 days' is implicit
Business impact analysis is comprehensive, correctly identifying the trust risk from guarantee/best-effort mismatch and the operational complexity of the 15-minute pickup SLA
BR-4 driver rating threshold classified as invariant:false — debatable, as Operations stated it as a hard quality gate, though the justification for policy flexibility is reasonable

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as first escalation with detailed options and recommendation; (2) Clock-start ambiguity identified in assumptions and edge cases ('Driver picks up at minute 14 but delivery misses window'); (3) Wrong-address exception from Finance captured as BR-6 with invariant=true and in ubiquitous language; (4) Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted in second escalation — highlights tension between automatic refunds (CS) and goodwill framing (Legal); (5) 9 business rules extracted with reasonable invariant classifications; (6) 2 formal escalations with affected stakeholders, options, and recommendations; (7) confidence.level=40, well below the 50 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptional escalation quality — both escalations include affected stakeholders, concrete options, and a nuanced recommendation rather than just flagging the problem
9 business rules extracted (exceeds minimum of 5) with thoughtful invariant justifications explaining why each is or isn't invariant
Edge cases are thorough and practical — double-refund overlap, near-miss timing, gated community ambiguity, warehouse outage, and address change scenarios
Confidence reasoning is well-articulated with clear separation of high-confidence and low-confidence areas plus explicit assumptions
Business impact analysis covers financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with specific stakeholder-by-stakeholder breakdown

Clock-start ambiguity is addressed but could have been a standalone escalation rather than buried in assumptions and edge cases — it is a genuine stakeholder disagreement (PM implies order placement, Ops implies pickup)

BR-3 (4-star driver rating) classified as invariant=false — the rubric expected invariant=true as a quality gate. The agent's reasoning (could be relaxed during shortages) is defensible but diverges from expected classification

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) PM/Legal guarantee contradiction explicitly identified and formally escalated as first escalation with three options and a recommendation; (2) Clock-start ambiguity identified — listed as an assumption ('2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup') and the 15-minute pickup eating into the 2-hour window is noted in BR-2's justification; (3) Wrong-address refund exception from Finance captured as BR-6 with testable assertion; (4) Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict identified and formally escalated as second escalation, including the interaction with wrong-address exclusion; (5) 9 business rules extracted with reasonable invariant classifications; (6) 2 formal escalations with options and recommendations; (7) confidence.level = 40, well under the 50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal contradiction is thoroughly analyzed with three concrete resolution options and a clear recommendation — exceeds minimum escalation requirements
Second escalation goes beyond the rubric by identifying the interaction between auto-refund, 3-day processing, AND wrong-address exclusion as a three-way tension
9 business rules extracted (rubric requires 5), all with testable assertions in Given/When/Then format
Rich edge cases section (9 scenarios) surfaces ambiguities not explicitly in the rubric — multiple warehouse distance, consecutive driver cancellations, dynamic availability
Confidence reasoning directly ties the low score to the unresolved guarantee contradiction affecting the entire domain model
Business impact analysis identifies the trust risk of showing guarantee language while legally disclaiming it
Clock-start ambiguity is captured as an assumption rather than a formal escalation — the rubric expected this to be escalated since PM implies from order placement while Ops implies from pickup

BR-3 (4-star driver rating) classified as invariant:false — rubric expected invariant:true as a quality gate. The agent's reasoning ('VP could lower it') is defensible but diverges from expected classification
